name: checkpoint-rewind
description: User rewinds to an earlier auto-checkpoint, restoring files and/or conversation; subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point. Each adapter exercises this differently: claudecode rewinds IN PLACE (Esc+Esc shortcut / /rewind picker) and mutates its transcript backwards, keeping the SAME session_id — the goal there is to capture what irrlicht observes when the append-oriented transcript_tailer sees a truncation (session-removed-then-reborn vs. activity). codex instead FORKS (/fork): it spawns a NEW rollout with a fresh session_id carrying the replayed pre-fork history (the original lingers frozen), so irrlicht observes a new linked session row rather than an in-place truncation. Every variant drives two normal turns, rewinds, then runs a third turn from the rewound point.

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The canonical primitive is a *checkpoint/branch* capability: rewind to an EARLIER checkpoint (one of several), restoring files and/or conversation, then continue cleanly from the rewound point. Aider 0.86.2 has no such primitive. Enumerating every slash command in the installed package (`aider/commands.py`, all `def cmd_*` methods) yields `/undo`, `/reset`, `/clear` — and NO `/rewind`, `/checkpoint`, or `/fork`. The aider docs confirm the same surface (https://aider.chat/docs/usage/commands.html).

What aider DOES have, and why this is `partial` rather than `no`:

- **`/undo`** — "Undo the last git commit if it was done by aider" (`raw_cmd_undo`). It reverts ONLY the single most-recent aider commit (it bails with `tool_error` if the last commit isn't in `self.coder.aider_commit_hashes`, if it was already pushed, or has >1 parent). This is a degenerate one-step case of the rewind primitive: it restores FILES to the previous commit, but does not touch conversation state and offers no multi-point picker.
- **`/reset`** — drops all files AND clears chat history. That is the `session-reset` neighbour (explicitly called out under "Not to be confused with"), not a rewind.
- **`/clear`** — clears chat history only. Also session-reset territory.

Because the closest match (`/undo`) covers only files, only the last commit, and only when aider made it, aider partially supports the primitive. There is no branch/fork and no arbitrary-checkpoint restore.

## daemon_capability: incapable

The split puts the blocker on the daemon-observability axis, not the driver. The aider adapter is `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser}` (core/.../aider/agent.go). One markdown transcript per project CWD; there is NO session_id rotation and no second transcript file — so a "linked new session_id" (the fork branch of the scenario's observable signal) is structurally impossible for aider.

The in-place branch of the signal also produces no distinguishing event. Aider's `InputOutput.get_input()` logs EVERY raw line — including slash commands — to the chat history via `self.user_input(inp)` (io.py:733) before dispatching, so a `/undo` lands in the transcript as a `#### /undo` line. The irrlicht parser maps any `#### …` line to a turn-opening `user_message` event (parser.go ParseLineRaw, line 94); the `/undo` success blockquote (`io.tool_output` → `> …`) falls through to the ignored-status branch (parser.go line 126); IdleFlush then synthesizes `turn_done` (parser.go line 216). Net effect: `/undo` surfaces as an ordinary `ready → working → ready` arc on the SAME session, identical in shape to a normal turn. The git HEAD movement is invisible because the adapter watches the markdown transcript, not the repo. The only trace that a rewind happened is the literal string `/undo` inside a `####` line — and the parser does not classify slash-command markers as a distinct lifecycle event.

So none of the scenario's user-observable signals (rewound-state reflection, same-vs-new session_id distinction, a detectable rewind) can be represented: the rewind leaves no trace the daemon classifies as a rewind, and the 3-state model has no event to express the rollback. That is the `incapable` bucket — no trace / no representation — NOT `bug`: there is no spec-required observable that the parser receives and mis-handles; the distinguishing signal genuinely never reaches the watched Source. (Same precedent as the claudecode checkpoint-rewind caveat: a rewind is invisible to file-watching.)

## driver_capability: ready

The interactive driver (scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh) implements `send`, `slash`, `wait_turn`, `interrupt`, `sleep`. The `/undo` recipe needs only `send` (drive a turn that produces a file edit + aider commit) plus `slash` (issue `/undo`) plus `wait_turn`/`sleep` — all present. None of the driver's gap primitives (restart, reset_session, resume, start_session, exit_clean, sigkill, keys, session, fork) is required for the `/undo` path, so the driver is `ready`. The blocker is purely the daemon-observability axis above.

## Capability key

The Primitive exercised ("Checkpoint/branch capability … maps to `checkpoint_rewind` in `capabilities.json` when present") maps to a `checkpoint_rewind` key. Aider's `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` has NO `checkpoint_rewind` key (and correctly so) — confirming the absence of a checkpoint primitive at the capability level. The `/undo` mechanism rides on the existing `git_integration: true` key, not a dedicated checkpoint primitive.

## Matrix agreement

This ratifies the existing coverage cell (`agent_supports: partial`, `daemon_capability: incapable`, `driver_capability: ready`) and the `scenarios.json` recipe scope note (`applicable: false`, recording deferred). No drift.
caveats: `/undo` is files-only and single-step: it reverts just the last aider-authored git commit (refuses if not aider's, already pushed, or a merge). It is NOT a multi-point checkpoint picker and does not restore conversation state — so even the supported slice is a narrow, degenerate case of the rewind primitive.

feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: a `/undo` is observable only as the literal `#### /undo` text inside an otherwise normal ready→working→ready turn; irrlicht emits no rewind-specific lifecycle event and never sees the git HEAD movement, because the FilesUnderCWD adapter watches the markdown transcript, not the repo.

A forked/new-session_id rewind (the codex-style branch of the observable signal) is structurally impossible for aider: FilesUnderCWD binds one fixed transcript file per project CWD, so there is no second session row to link to.

sources.note: Command reference: /undo = 'Undo the last git commit if it was done by aider' (git-only, last commit); no /rewind, /checkpoint, or /fork documented
aider 0.86.2 full slash-command enumeration (def cmd_*): /undo, /reset, /clear present; no rewind/checkpoint/fork. raw_cmd_undo reverts only the last aider commit and bails otherwise

get_input() at line 733 logs every raw line (incl. slash commands) to .aider.chat.history.md via user_input(); tool_output() writes blockquote status lines — so /undo enters the transcript as a #### line

Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}: one fixed transcript per project CWD, no session_id rotation — a linked-new-session_id fork is structurally impossible (daemon_capability incapable, not a driver gap)

ParseLineRaw (line 94) maps any '#### …' line to a turn-opening user_message; blockquotes are ignored status (line 126); IdleFlush (line 216) synthesizes turn_done. /undo therefore yields a vanilla ready→working→ready arc indistinguishable from a normal turn — the rewind leaves no representable trace (incapable, not bug)
Driver implements send/slash/wait_turn/interrupt/sleep; the /undo recipe needs only send+slash+wait_turn — no gapped primitive required, so driver_capability is ready
No checkpoint_rewind key (closest is git_integration:true) — confirms aider has no checkpoint primitive at the capability level

recipe.scope_note: Coverage matrix marks aider supports=partial, observes=no. /undo reverts the last aider git commit and surfaces as a normal chat turn in the transcript; there is no multi-point checkpoint picker. Capturing it requires aider to first make a file edit and commit, then /undo — a recipe shape that needs file-tool granularity the current driver doesn't deterministically produce. Recording deferred.

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claude Code 2.1.x supports checkpoint rewind via `/rewind` (alias `/checkpoint`, also Esc+Esc): restores files and/or conversation to any earlier user prompt; auto-checkpointed per prompt. See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/checkpointing.

The canonical spec requires three observables, all of which irrlicht emits correctly:

1. **Session row reflects the rewound state with state `ready`** — yes. The rewind itself isn't a turn; the session stays in ready before, during, and after the picker UI is dismissed.
2. **Same `session_id` if the adapter rewinds in place** — yes. Claudecode keeps a linear `parentUuid` chain on the same transcript file and the same UUID throughout. The 2026-05-16 recording confirms this.
3. **Subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point: `ready → working → ready`** — yes. The post-rewind prompt drives the standard turn arc on the same UUID.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

The rewind ITSELF is invisible to irrlicht's file-watching surface. Claudecode keeps the linear transcript and handles the rewind purely in-TUI / in-memory — no `transcript_removed`, no `transcript_new`, no marker line. During the 10-second window when the `/rewind` picker is on screen, irrlicht's events.jsonl records zero events.

That's fine: the spec doesn't require detecting the rewind, only that state remains correct after one. Which it does.

## Caveat: context-utilization drift

The one place rewind invisibility leaks into user-facing data is the context_utilization_percentage / pressure_level chip. Irrlicht counts tokens from the full transcript on disk; claude only re-feeds the post-rewind subset into its next prompt. So after a rewind, irrlicht slightly OVERSTATES how full claude's context actually is. Cumulative cost remains accurate (past API calls were real spend).

This is a minor metric-accuracy nit, not a state correctness issue. Tracked in the caveats field below.
caveats: Rewind events are invisible to irrlicht's file-watching surface (claudecode handles rewinds in-TUI without modifying the transcript). Spec compliance is unaffected — state remains correct after a rewind — but irrlicht cannot DETECT that a rewind happened.

Context utilization % and pressure level may slightly overstate after a rewind: irrlicht sums tokens from the full transcript on disk while claude only re-feeds the post-rewind subset into its next prompt. Cumulative cost remains accurate.

sources.note: /rewind docs — alias /checkpoint, also Esc+Esc
2026-05-16 recording: 3 clean ready→working→ready arcs on same UUID, linear parentUuid chain throughout, zero events during the 10s rewind-picker window

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
claude version with /rewind support (Esc+Esc shortcut) — confirmed in 2.1.x
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
no other claudecode session uses the same UUID
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: one
Reply with exactly the word: two
Reply with exactly the word: three

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the UUID session within 500 ms of the first send
events.jsonl contains ≥ 2 state_transition working entries (one per pre-rewind turn)
events.jsonl contains ≥ 2 state_transition ready entries returning from those turns
After Esc+Esc, the transcript file is mutated (truncated or rewritten); inspect events.jsonl for transcript_removed / transcript_new on the SAME session_id vs. just transcript_activity
Post-rewind turn shows a clean ready → working → ready arc on the rewound session
Final session state is ready
transcript.jsonl shows turns 1/2 then a structural break (truncation or marker) then turn 3

daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

This re-judges from primary evidence on the installed codex-cli 0.130.0 and confirms the 2026-05-28 reconciled verdict. The older `no / n.a.` block that previously sat beneath the reconciled verdict in the shard is dropped — it was wrong (see "Why not `no`" below).

## What the scenario asks

The scenario-meaning's User-observable signal is explicit that adapters exercise checkpoint-rewind differently: "same `session_id` if rewound in place, **or a linked new `session_id` if the adapter forks**; turns after the rewind follow a clean arc." codex is the fork variant. The signal to observe is therefore a NEW session row in `ready` after the rewind, with a clean `ready → working → ready` arc on the next turn.

## agent_supports: partial

codex 0.130.0 DOES ship a rewind affordance, but a conversation-only one:

- `/fork` is a live TUI slash command. The live command palette (codex v0.130.0, captured this session) shows `/fork  fork the current chat`, and `--help` lists a top-level `codex fork` subcommand ("Fork a previous interactive session"). Official docs: `/fork` "Clone the current conversation into a new thread with a fresh ID, leaving the original transcript untouched."
- Esc-Esc on an empty composer walks the transcript back to edit a prior user message (TUI backtrack), then Enter forks from that point.

It is `partial` (not `yes`) because the fork reverts CONVERSATION state only — prior file edits remain in the working tree ("the conversation rewinds, but prior file edits remain"). Native file-edit checkpoint restore is an OPEN feature request (#11626, #12558), not shipped. So codex covers the conversation half of "files and/or conversation" but not the file half.

## Why not `no`

The superseded `no / n.a.` verdict argued checkpoint-rewind "means rolling back FILE and/or conversation state" and that `/fork` is "branching, not rollback." That overreaches: (1) the scenario-meaning explicitly includes the fork case as a valid variant ("a linked new `session_id` if the adapter forks"); (2) "files AND/OR conversation" is satisfied by the conversation arm alone. A conversation fork that restores the pre-fork history into a fresh session IS the rewind this cell is about for a forking adapter. The honest downgrade is `partial` (no file revert), not `no`.

Further confirmations that there is no SECOND, file-reverting rewind being missed: the live palette returns no completion for `/rewind` or `/undo` in 0.130.0; `/undo` was removed (discussion #9618). The `/rewind` token present in the native binary's string table is dormant — it is not a live TUI command.

## daemon_capability: full

The codex Source is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: sessionsDir()}` watching `~/.codex/sessions/**.jsonl` (adapter.go:21, agent.go:32). A `/fork` EAGERLY materializes a NEW rollout-<ts>-<uuid2>.jsonl under that watched root — confirmed by a real on-disk rollout (`019e5615-9632-...`) whose first line is `{"type":"session_meta","payload":{"id":"019e5615-9632-...","forked_from_id":"019e5615-1ef8-..."}}`. A new file under the watched root is exactly what produces a new session row in `ready`, and the post-fork turn's `working→ready` cycle is on that new rollout. The user-observable signal — a linked new session_id with a clean arc — is therefore emitted by the daemon as it exists today. The original rollout is NOT removed at fork time (codex emits no transcript_removed; the old file lingers), so the original row stays `ready` until process_exited; the scenario verify[] already accounts for this.

The `forked_from_id` provenance is dropped: the codex parser skips `session_meta`/`turn_context` (parser.go:179-180) and nothing in the codex adapter populates `ParentSessionID`. This means the daemon surfaces the fork as an INDEPENDENT new session, not as a parent-linked child. Under the caveats-over-downgrades rule this stays `full` + caveat, NOT `bug`: the scenario's spec-required observable is "a (linked) new session_id appears with a clean arc," which IS satisfied — the new row appears and transitions correctly. The lost linkage LABEL is a narrow provenance gap the spec does not require, so it is a caveat. (Same provenance-drop shape as codex/session-reset, where supersession is still observed.)

## driver_capability: ready

The shard recipe drives the fork with `send` / `fork` / `wait_turn` / `sleep` steps only — every one of which the codex interactive driver implements (fork is sent as text+Enter; no keys/restart/resume/sigkill primitives are needed). Esc-Esc backtrack is deliberately avoided in the recipe because the driver's interrupt step desyncs the turn counter, but that is a recipe choice, not a missing primitive. No `gap:*`.

## Primitive / capability key

Primitive exercised: "Checkpoint/branch capability ... Maps to `checkpoint_rewind` in `capabilities.json` when present." The legacy per-adapter `capabilities.json` was deleted in the #511 shard migration, so there is no key file to confirm against; the canonical primitive name is `checkpoint_rewind`.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant (provenance only): the parser skips session_meta (parser.go:179-180) and nothing sets ParentSessionID, so the daemon shows the fork as an independent new session_id, not a parent-linked child — the new row + clean arc are observed, only the forked_from_id linkage label is lost.
/fork reverts conversation state only; prior file edits stay in the working tree (file-revert checkpoint restore is open request #11626/#12558, unshipped) — hence agent_supports: partial, not yes.

known capture gap (driver): drive-codex-interactive.sh caches the first rollout and curate-lifecycle-fixture.sh filters to UUID-1, so the post-/fork (UUID-2) phases may be absent from the single-session curated fixture and surface as validation drift until the driver tracks /fork-spawned sessions — same gap as codex/session-reset, a developer task, not an observability limit.

sources.note: /fork 'Clone the current conversation into a new thread with a fresh ID, leaving the original transcript untouched'; no /rewind or /undo documented; conversation-only scope.
OPEN request to add /rewind checkpoint restore that reverts BOTH chat context and Codex-applied code edits — proves file-revert is not shipped (basis for partial, not yes).
Discussion confirming the experimental /undo was removed and there is no /rewind/revert; rules out a second file-reverting rewind being missed.
Source is FilesUnderRoot{Dir: sessionsDir()}; a /fork's new rollout file lands under the watched root → new session row observable (daemon_capability: full).
Line 179-180 skips session_meta/turn_context; nothing sets ParentSessionID — forked_from_id provenance is dropped (the caveat behind partial, not a bug).
Real codex rollout: first line is session_meta with payload.forked_from_id pointing at the parent — direct evidence the fork writes a new watched file carrying linkage the parser then drops.

sources.ref: live: codex v0.130.0 TUI command palette (captured 2026-05-29 via tmux)
sources.note: Palette shows '/fork  fork the current chat'; '/rewind' and '/undo' yield no completion — confirms /fork is the only live rewind affordance in the installed build.

preconditions: codex CLI on PATH and authenticated (~/.codex/config.toml or OPENAI_API_KEY) — verified codex 0.130.0
codex version with /fork support — verified in 0.130.0 (TUI prints 'Thread forked from <uuid>')
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record

STALE-VERDICT OVERRIDE: the prior by_adapter.codex was applicable:false on the false belief codex 0.130 has no rewind. Codex DOES support rewind/fork: /fork clones the conversation into a NEW thread (fresh session id; original intact), and Esc-Esc on an empty composer walks the transcript back to edit a prior message. Feature requests #11626/#12558 ask codex to ALSO revert code edits — orthogonal to the conversation fork that already ships. See assessment.json.

PARTIAL observability (per assessment.json): codex forks rather than rewinding in place, so the observable is a NEW linked session_id (not the same session_id with a truncated transcript like claudecode). The codex parser skips session_meta lines, so the forked_from_id provenance is dropped and the daemon sees the fork as an independent fresh session, not a linked child. codex emits NO transcript_removed for the original at fork time (the old rollout lingers frozen until process_exited).

KNOWN CAPTURE GAP: drive-codex-interactive.sh caches the first rollout and writes a single session.uuid, so curate-lifecycle-fixture.sh filters the fixture to UUID-1 only. The v2_* (post-/fork) phases assert what the daemon observes LIVE; they may be absent from the single-session curated fixture, surfacing as validation drift until the codex driver tracks /fork-spawned sessions (a developer task). SAME gap as codex/session-reset.
setup: Driver launches codex in a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (isolates the trust dialog).

Codex assigns its own session UUID per rollout; there is no --session-id flag. The first prompt materializes rollout-<ts>-<uuid1>.jsonl; /fork EAGERLY materializes a second rollout-<ts>-<uuid2>.jsonl carrying a fresh session_id, a session_meta forked_from_id=uuid1, and the replayed pre-fork history. UNLIKE /clear (lazy: second rollout appears only on the first post-clear prompt), /fork is eager — the new rollout appears the instant the command runs.

/fork is sent as a plain send/slash step (text + Enter): the codex driver handles send/slash as text+Enter. NO keys/restart/resume step types are used — the codex driver does not implement them. Esc-Esc rewind is deliberately avoided because the driver's interrupt step decrements the turn counter, which would desync wait_turn.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: one
Reply with exactly the word: two
Reply with exactly the word: three

verify: events.jsonl shows a proc-<PID> pre-session entering ready, handing off to the FIRST rollout-keyed session in ready
events.jsonl shows the FIRST rollout session running two working→ready turns (turn 1 'one', turn 2 'two') — the checkpoints to fork back from

after /fork, a DIFFERENT rollout session_id appears in ready (new linked session row, restored from the rewound point) — the 'a linked new session_id if the adapter forks' signal from scenario-meanings, observed live on the dashboard
the post-fork turn's working→ready cycle is on the NEW rollout session, not the original — a clean ready → working → ready arc from the rewound point

codex does NOT emit transcript_removed for the original session at /fork time (the old rollout lingers frozen) — the original row stays ready until process_exited; do not assert a removal event, and do not assert a same-session_id in-place rewind (codex forks)
final live state is ready

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

This re-derivation (opencode v1.14.50, current daemon, current drive-opencode-interactive.sh) OVERTURNS the prior verdict on two of the three axes. The prior cell said daemon=full (UNTESTED LIVE) and driver=gap:slash-command on the theory that (a) /undo "removes subsequent messages from the SQLite session" producing a transcript shrink-then-grow, and (b) the driver had no way to deliver an in-TUI slash command. Both premises are now false, proven by a live tmux-driven probe against opencode v1.14.50.

## Scenario

checkpoint-rewind: two normal turns, then the user rewinds the conversation/files to an earlier checkpoint and continues. User-observable signals (scenario-meanings): the row is `ready` across the rewind (a rewind is not a turn); the post-rewind turn is a clean `ready → working → ready`; and the SAME session_id is retained for an in-place rewind (vs. a linked new session_id for a forking adapter like codex).

## Agent supports it (agent_supports: yes)

opencode's TUI ships `/undo` and `/redo` as first-class slash commands: the binary defines `command.session.undo` (slash:"undo", title "Undo", description "undo the last message") and `command.session.redo` (slash:"redo", "Redo"). The footer help string reads "Use /undo to revert the last message and file changes" / "Use /redo to restore previously undone messages."

Mechanism (read from the binary AND confirmed live): `/undo` calls the `POST /session/{sessionID}/revert` API, which sets the session row's `revert` JSON column to `{messageID, snapshot, diff}` and restores the working tree via `Snapshot.restore(revert.snapshot)` (a git snapshot). It does NOT delete message/part rows — the reverted messages stay in the store and the TUI simply filters them (`revert?.messageID ? msgs.filter(m => m.id < messageID) : msgs`). `/redo` (`unrevert`) clears the marker and re-restores. On the NEXT user prompt, opencode trims messages with `id >= revert.messageID` and proceeds. This is an IN-PLACE rewind on the same `ses_…` (NOT a fork — unlike codex's `/fork`), restoring BOTH conversation and files — the strongest form of the canonical checkpoint primitive.

Live proof (tmux, v1.14.50, gemma-4-e2b local model): turn 1 completed → session row `revert` NULL, 2 messages / 4 parts. Sent `/undo` via `tmux send-keys -l -- /undo` + Enter → session row `revert` set to `{"messageID":"msg_…","snapshot":"1206cb66…","diff":""}`, message/part counts UNCHANGED (still 2/4 — no row deletion), prompt restored to the input box. A post-rewind turn → `revert` cleared, a 2nd step-finish landed (8 parts total), SAME `ses_…` retained, still exactly 1 top-level session in the cwd (no fork).

## daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter (core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go); the daemon reads session state from the SQLite store. The watcher's `scanParts` (watcher.go) is append-only: `WHERE p.time_updated >= lastTS`, terminal turn detected via a `step-finish` part with a stop/interrupted/length/error reason. The rewind itself sets the `session.revert` column and does NOT write any part rows, so the daemon emits no spurious activity for the rewind (the row stays `ready`). The post-rewind turn writes fresh parts with new `time_updated`, which the watcher observes exactly like any normal turn → `working`, then the `step-finish` → `ready`. Same `ses_…` throughout. Every state-correctness observable the spec asserts (ready across rewind; clean ready→working→ready after; same session_id) is precisely what the basic-turn and multi-turn opencode cells already prove the daemon surfaces — and the live probe confirms the rewind adds nothing the watcher mis-handles.

The prior "untested live / message-count DECREASE" worry is resolved: the store row NEVER shrinks. `/undo` is a column flip (`revert`), not a part deletion. There is no monotonicity violation for the daemon to choke on. daemon_capability is full, no longer caveated as untested.

## driver can record it (driver_capability: ready)

The prior gap:slash-command verdict is STALE. The current drive-opencode-interactive.sh now has a `run_live()` path that drives the opencode TUI under tmux and a generic `slash` step type that does `tmux send-keys -l -- "$s"` + Enter against the live held-open session (lines 205-210); the top-level router sends any script containing a `reset_session` OR `slash` step down `run_live()` (lines 355-357). Turn detection polls `step-finish` parts across the cwd's sessions in the SQLite store, and the multi-session export emits the session.uuids / transcript.paths contract. I verified the slash primitive end-to-end: `tmux send-keys -- /undo` against a live v1.14.50 TUI performed the rewind (set `session.revert`). A recipe of `send / wait_turn` (turn 1, turn 2) → `slash /undo` → `send / wait_turn` (turn 3) is fully expressible in the driver's grammar today. Route: record.

## Caveats (metric-drift, not a daemon bug)

ComputeMetrics (metrics.go querySessionMetrics) folds ALL parts (`ORDER BY time_created ASC`) with no awareness of the `session.revert` marker. Because reverted parts are NOT deleted, the daemon's cost/token/last-assistant-text metrics after a rewind reflect the FULL pre-rewind history (reverted turns included) plus the new turn — they do not roll back to the rewound view. This is a metric-drift-downstream caveat, NOT a `bug`: the spec's user-observable signals are state/session_id/arc (all correct), and cost/tokens are explicitly out-of-scope for the rewind primitive. Keep daemon_capability: full and surface this as a caveat.

## Capability key

The scenario's Primitive exercised ("Checkpoint/branch capability … Maps to `checkpoint_rewind` in capabilities.json when present") has no `checkpoint_rewind` key in replaydata/agents/opencode/capabilities.json (claudecode's lacks it too). The scenario gates on `requires: [headless_mode]`, which opencode declares true. No capability-key correction is needed.

## Matrix drift

The coverage row currently reads agent_supports:yes / daemon:full / driver:gap:slash-command (note: "daemon observability untested; needs a live-REPL opencode driver (tracked: #492)"). This re-assessment flips driver to `ready` (the live-REPL slash driver now EXISTS and is proven), drops the "untested" qualifier on daemon (live-confirmed full), and corrects the mechanism note (in-place revert-column rewind, NOT message removal). The maintainer should update the coverage cell to driver_capability:ready and re-note. #492 (live-REPL opencode driver) is the work that landed the run_live()/slash path that unblocks this cell — its closure should be checked against this re-assessment.

## Confidence

0.85: the agent mechanism is read directly from the binary (command.session.undo/redo, session.revert column, Snapshot.restore) AND confirmed by a live tmux probe (revert column set on /undo, same ses_, no fork, clean post-rewind turn); the daemon path is read from watcher.go + metrics.go and matches the live store mutation; the driver slash step is read from the live driver source and exercised against a live TUI. Not 0.9+ because a full promoted recording of the three-turn-plus-/undo recipe through run-cell.sh has not yet been captured (this re-assessment authorizes that recording, route: record).
caveats: record_blocked=driver_bug (2026-05-28): the driver has the needed step types (recipe-lint passes — NOT a gap; the body section 'driver can record it' verifies the slash primitive end-to-end against a live TUI) and the daemon faithfully records what fires (NOT a daemon bug), but drive-opencode-interactive.sh has a run_live slash→send composer-flush hand-off race under the slow local model: capturing the send→send→/undo→send sequence has the /undo coalesce with the next prompt (4 attempts across 2026-05-27 and 2026-05-28, never clean). Re-record after the hand-off is hardened (oc_flush confirms a settled empty composer around the slash step) or with a faster model. driver_capability stays `ready` (the steps exist); the blocker is encoded as record_blocked=driver_bug per the assess/SKILL.md routing (PR #507). Reconciliation of #506's `gap:slash-command` field/text inconsistency.

Metric-drift (not a daemon bug): opencode's /undo sets the session.revert column and does NOT delete the reverted message/part rows. The daemon's ComputeMetrics folds ALL parts with no revert-awareness, so post-rewind cost/token/last-text metrics reflect the full pre-rewind history plus the new turn, not the rewound view. State/session_id/arc — the spec's user-observable signals — are all correct; cost/tokens are out of scope for this primitive, so daemon_capability stays full.

agent_supports is yes (not partial): /undo restores BOTH conversation AND working-tree files (via a git snapshot), in place on the same ses_ — the full canonical checkpoint primitive, stronger than claudecode's conversation-only in-place rewind, and unlike codex which forks to a new session_id.

sources.note: FLIPS the prior gap:slash-command verdict: run_live() (lines 99-294) drives the opencode TUI under tmux, the generic `slash` step (lines 205-210) does `tmux send-keys -l -- "$s"` + Enter against a live held-open session, and the router (lines 355-357) sends any script with a slash/reset_session step down run_live(). A send/wait_turn + slash /undo + send/wait_turn recipe is expressible today.

scanParts is append-only (WHERE p.time_updated >= lastTS) with terminal detection on a step-finish part (stop/interrupted/length/error). The rewind sets the session.revert column and writes NO part rows, so the row stays ready; the post-rewind turn's fresh parts drive a clean working→ready on the same ses_. The watcher never reads session.revert and never sees a part deletion.

querySessionMetrics folds ALL parts (ORDER BY time_created ASC) with no session.revert awareness — anchors the metric-drift caveat: post-rewind cost/tokens reflect the full pre-rewind history because reverted parts are not deleted.
opencode Source is ProcessOwnedStore (line 41); the daemon reads SQLite session state keyed on ses_. An in-place /undo mutates the session.revert column, not part rows.

command.session.undo (slash:"undo"), command.session.redo (slash:"redo"); footer "Use /undo to revert the last message and file changes"; POST /session/{sessionID}/revert sets session.revert={messageID,snapshot,diff} and Snapshot.restore(revert.snapshot) restores files; reverted messages are filtered (m.id < messageID), NOT deleted. session table has a `revert text` column (confirmed in the live DB schema).

sources.ref: live tmux probe against opencode v1.14.50 (gemma-4-e2b local model), 2026-05-26
sources.note: turn1 → revert NULL, 2 msg/4 parts; tmux send-keys -- /undo → session.revert set to {messageID:msg_…, snapshot:1206cb66…, diff:""}, msg/part counts UNCHANGED (no deletion), prompt restored to input; post-rewind turn → revert cleared, 2nd step-finish landed (8 parts), SAME ses_ retained, exactly 1 top-level session in cwd (no fork). Proves agent_supports:yes (in-place rewind), daemon:full (clean arc, no shrink), driver:ready (slash /undo delivered live).

preconditions: opencode on PATH and authenticated (~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json) — verified opencode 1.14.50. The checkpoint-rewind arc is model-agnostic; the dev env's default is a local LM Studio model, which is fine.
irrlichd is recording — run-cell.sh isolated mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record.

LIVE-TUI cell: drive-opencode-interactive.sh routes any recipe carrying a `slash` step to its tmux TUI path, because headless `opencode run` stores a slash command as literal prompt text (PROBED 2026-05-26: `opencode run "/undo"` would store `/undo` as a user message rather than performing the rewind). tmux must be on PATH.
opencode version with /undo support — confirmed v1.14.50 (command.session.undo, slash 'undo'; footer 'Use /undo to revert the last message and file changes').
setup: Driver launches the opencode TUI in a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/; opencode keys sessions on the cwd in its SQLite store, isolating this run's rows even alongside other opencode use.

opencode mints its own ses_… for the session; all three turns AND the /undo rewind happen IN PLACE on that one ses_… (no fork — unlike codex's /fork). The driver enumerates the cwd's top-level ses_… rows; an in-place rewind leaves exactly one, so the multi-session contract (session.uuids / transcript.paths) carries a single session.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: one
Reply with exactly the word: two
Reply with exactly the word: three

verify: events.jsonl shows the ses_… session entering ready, then turn 1 (working→ready, 'one') and turn 2 (working→ready, 'two') on the SAME ses_…

after /undo the session stays ready — a rewind is not a turn; opencode sets the session.revert column and restores files via a git snapshot but writes no part rows, so no state_transition distinguishes the rewind from a dwell (assert a ready dwell, not a working/waiting flip)
the message/part rows are NOT deleted (revert is a column flip) — do NOT assert any message/part-count shrink; same ses_… throughout, no new session_id minted (in-place rewind, not a fork)
turn 3 ('three') is a clean ready→working→ready arc on the SAME ses_…, issued from the rewound point
exactly one process_exited on teardown; final live state is ready
recipe.notes: DEGRADED applicable:false again (2026-05-27, opencode v1.14.50, run-cell.sh COEXIST mode on :7838, local gemma-4-e2b model) after TWO consecutive recording attempts reproduced the SAME composer-coalescing defect — even with both prior driver fixes (f2cabf08 terminal step-finish counting + f5b15758 composer-flush confirmation) IN PLACE and live on HEAD. The first two turns serialize cleanly (terminal step-finish 0→1, 1→2; no premature completion), but the `slash` /undo step that follows turn 2 is SWALLOWED ENTIRELY and the turn-2 + turn-3 prompts COALESCE into one user message — both runs' staged transcripts show the identical merged literal 'Reply with exactly the word: twoReply with exactly the word: three' with NO /undo text and only 2 terminal step-finishes, so wait_turn for turn 3 times out (total stuck at 2). PRECISE REMAINING BLOCKER: under this slow local model (turn 1 ~100s to first token), f5b15758's per-send oc_flush capture-pane check is necessary but NOT sufficient across the send→slash→send sequence — the turn-2 send's composer is reported flushed (its turn fires) yet residual composer state lets the subsequent `slash` /undo + turn-3 send merge into the tail of turn 2, dropping /undo. The earlier 'live-verified' probe ended AT the /undo (send→wait_turn→send→wait_turn→slash) with no following turn-3 send, so it never exercised the slash→send hand-off that races here. This is a driver TUI-composer race (oc_send/slash do not reliably isolate the slash step + the turn after it from the prior send under slow-model latency), NOT a missing step type (recipe-lint passes; not a SKILL driver_gap) and NOT a daemon bug (the daemon faithfully recorded the 2 arcs that actually fired). Fix path: harden oc_flush so the `slash` arm and the send immediately AFTER a slash each confirm a SETTLED, empty composer (and that the prior turn's spinner cleared) before typing — e.g. wait for the spinner to vanish AND the composer prefix to clear, with a poll keyed to model latency, so /undo lands on its own and turn 3 is its own submission; then re-record. expected.jsonl stays as the documented in-place-rewind target (state/session_id/arc only — same ses_, revert set, no fork, clean post-rewind turn; never message/part counts).

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:keys

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:keys

(Refines the prior matrix cell, which read `no` / `n/a` against pi 0.69.x where stock Pi shipped no rewind — pi-mono discussion #1223. pi 0.70.0 changed this.)

## Reasoning

pi 0.70.0 DOES ship an in-REPL rewind: the `/tree` slash command opens an interactive tree-selector overlay (`dist/modes/interactive/components/tree-selector.js`), and selecting an earlier entry calls `session.navigateTree(entryId)` → `SessionManager.branch(branchFromId)`. The mechanism is an APPEND-ONLY tree: `branch()` only moves the in-memory `leafId` pointer; the source comment is explicit — "Existing entries are not modified or deleted." The next `appendXXX()` (the next turn) writes a child of the rewound entry into the SAME `.jsonl` session file under the SAME `session_id`. There is no `/clone`-style new file at rewind time (that is a separate `createBranchedSession` path, not what `/tree` selection does). So pi rewinds IN PLACE, like claudecode — not a fork like codex.

Why `agent_supports: partial` rather than `yes`: the spec's canonical primitive is a files-and-conversation checkpoint restore. pi restores CONVERSATION state (leaf pointer) but does not snapshot or restore the working-tree FILES — there is no shadow-git / file-checkpoint layer in stock pi. The conversation half is present; the file-restore half is not. Hence partial.

## What irrlicht observes (and doesn't)

pi's adapter is `FilesUnderRoot` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/agent.go`: `Source: FilesUnderRoot{Dir: sessionsDir(), Parser: JSONLineParser{...}}`) — it tails the append-only session JSONL.

Mapping the scenario's three user-observable signals:

1. **Session row state `ready` after rewind** — observable, but trivially: the rewind is not a turn, so the session sits in `ready` throughout the picker.
2. **Same `session_id` (in-place rewind)** — TRUE for pi (append-only, same file/UUID), and would be observable on the post-rewind turn.
3. **Post-rewind turn `ready → working → ready`** — observable as a standard turn arc on the same session_id, IF a turn follows.

The rewind EVENT ITSELF is invisible to file-watching: `branch()` writes nothing at selection time — no truncation, no `transcript_removed`, no `transcript_new`, no session_id change, no marker line. The tailer sees the post-rewind turn as ordinary append activity; nothing distinguishes a rewind from normal forward progress. This is the same invisibility claudecode has (see claudecode/checkpoint-rewind assessment), so the cell is at best `partial`, never `yes` on the observe axis.

## Why this cell is not recordable today (driver gap)

Reaching and SELECTING a checkpoint inside the `/tree` overlay is keyboard-driven: `tree-selector.js#handleInput` consumes `tui.select.up` / `tui.select.down` (arrow keys) to move the highlight and `tui.select.confirm` (Enter) to commit `onSelect`. The pi interactive driver (`.claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scripts/drive-pi-interactive.sh`) implements only `send` / `slash` (type-text-and-Enter), `wait_turn`, `interrupt`, and `sleep` — it has NO `keys` step type for arrow-key picker navigation. Sending the literal text `/tree` opens the picker but the driver cannot then move the selection or confirm a non-default entry, so the rewind can never be exercised deterministically. Extending the driver with a `keys` step is a developer task, out of scope for this cell — so the cell is frozen as a driver gap, not recorded.

## What would unblock it

Add a `keys` step type to `drive-pi-interactive.sh` (emit `tmux send-keys Up/Down/Enter` against the picker). Once the driver can navigate the `/tree` overlay, this cell becomes recordable as a `partial` observe — the post-rewind turn arc on the same session_id is the observable surface; the rewind event itself remains undetectable (document as a caveat, exactly as for claudecode).
caveats: The rewind event itself is invisible to irrlicht's file-watch transport: pi's branch() only moves an in-memory leaf pointer and writes nothing at selection time, so no transcript transition distinguishes a rewind from normal forward progress. Spec state-correctness is unaffected (the post-rewind turn arc on the same session_id is observable), but irrlicht cannot DETECT that a rewind happened.

agent_supports is partial, not yes: pi restores conversation state (leaf pointer) but has no file/shadow-git checkpoint, so the working-tree-files half of the canonical checkpoint primitive is absent.
Not recordable today: the /tree picker needs arrow-key + confirm keystrokes and the pi interactive driver has no `keys` step type.

LAZY-TRANSCRIPT CORRECTION (discovered 2026-05-28 during live recording): contrary to the earlier doc claim that pi is FilesUnderRoot non-lazy, pi actually creates its per-session .jsonl file only AFTER the first user message commits, with turn 1 user+assistant already batched into the file at creation time. The daemon`s transcript_new fires after the file exists, so no working→ready state_transition is emitted for the FIRST turn. The recipe sends three prompts (one/two/three) but only two turn arcs are daemon-observable (the recipe`s `two` turn and the post-rewind `three` turn). The spec was rewritten to use pre_rewind_turn + post_rewind_ready (bounded duration_at_least_ms dwell, same shape as claudecode/checkpoint-rewind) + post_rewind_turn instead of three explicit t1/t2/t3 arcs. The rewind itself remains unobservable (branch() writes nothing), which is the same observability shape claudecode has.

sources.note: branch(branchFromId) moves leafId only; 'Existing entries are not modified or deleted'; sessions are append-only JSONL trees (lines 419-843)
/tree handler (line 2022) → showTreeSelector → session.navigateTree(entryId) → branch() (line 3426ff); /tree is in-place rewind, distinct from /clone createBranchedSession
handleInput consumes tui.select.up/down (arrows) + tui.select.confirm (Enter) — picker navigation is keystroke-driven (line 763ff)
pi Source is FilesUnderRoot{Dir: sessionsDir(), Parser: JSONLineParser} — append-only transcript tail; no event emitted for an in-place leaf-pointer move
driver implements send/slash/wait_turn/interrupt/sleep only; no `keys` step for arrow-key picker navigation
anchor: in-place rewind invisible to file-watch but post-rewind arc observable — same shape pi would have if drivable

preconditions: pi CLI on PATH (verified pi 0.70.0) and authenticated (provider API key in env)
pi version with /tree in-place rewind — confirmed 0.70.0 (interactive-mode.js /tree → showTreeSelector → session.navigateTree → branch())
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record

PARTIAL observability (per assessment.json): the rewind event itself is invisible (branch() moves the in-memory leaf pointer and writes nothing at selection time); only the post-rewind turn arc on the SAME append-only session_id is observable
setup: Driver launches pi in a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/.

pi writes one append-only JSONL session file (FilesUnderRoot, non-lazy) under sessionsDir(); the same file + session_id persists across the rewind — branch() appends the next turn as a child of the rewound entry, it does not mint a new file.

/tree is sent as a slash step (text + Enter) to open the picker; the picker is then driven with `keys` steps (Up/Down to highlight the turn-1 checkpoint, Enter to confirm) using the raw tmux send-keys path provided by the `keys` step type.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: one
Reply with exactly the word: two
Reply with exactly the word: three

verify: events.jsonl shows a proc-<PID> pre-session entering ready, handing off to the rollout-keyed session in ready
the rollout session runs two working→ready turns (turn 1 'one', turn 2 'two') — the checkpoints to rewind to

after /tree selection, NO transcript_removed and NO transcript_new appear — the rewind is invisible to file-watching (branch() writes nothing at selection time); the session stays ready and on the SAME session_id
the post-rewind turn shows a clean ready → working → ready arc on the SAME rollout session_id (in-place rewind, not a fork) — the 'same session_id if rewound in place' signal from scenario-meanings
exactly one session_id appears across the whole recording (no fork); a single process_exited at teardown
final live state is ready
recipe.notes: pi 0.70.0 ships an in-REPL rewind via the /tree slash command: it opens an interactive tree-selector overlay whose handleInput consumes tui.select.up/down + tui.select.confirm; selecting an earlier entry calls session.navigateTree(entryId) → SessionManager.branch(branchFromId), which only moves the in-memory leafId pointer ("existing entries are not modified or deleted") and keeps the SAME append-only .jsonl session file under the SAME session_id — the next turn is appended as a child of the rewound entry. An in-place rewind, like claudecode (NOT a fork like codex). agent_supports=partial: pi restores conversation state but not the working-tree files (no shadow-git in stock pi). The rewind EVENT itself is invisible to file-watch — branch() writes nothing at selection time — so we assert it as a negative invariant over the dwell window between the /tree selection and turn 3. Recipe uses the `keys` step type (raw tmux send-keys) added in commit 88400160 to navigate the /tree picker: send /tree, sleep to let the overlay materialize, `Up Up` to walk back from the latest-highlighted entry to the turn-1 leaf, Enter to commit the rewind. If the live overlay layout differs (oldest-first instead of newest-first), adjust the `Up` count to whatever lands on turn-1. The picker shows newest-first highlighted at the latest entry; `Up Up` from (4/4) `assistant: two` lands on (2/4) `assistant: one` (the turn-1 leaf). The first Enter selects that leaf and pi pops up a `Summarize branch? → No summary / Summarize / Summarize with custom prompt` confirmation dialog; a SECOND Enter accepts the default `No summary` and surfaces `Navigated to selected point`. Only then is it safe to send turn 3 (otherwise the third send is consumed by the dialog).
